advantages: Covers open-ended language work—drafting, summarizing, explaining—that rigid scripts and keyword search handle poorly.
One assistant can span roles (writer, analyst, coder) when workflows are designed with clear inputs and reviewers.
API access lets teams embed the same capabilities into products (support copilots, doc Q&A) instead of one-off chat sessions.
Grounding with uploaded docs or retrieval makes enterprise FAQ and policy use cases practical without retraining the model.
disadvantages: High-stakes decisions (legal, medical, financial) still need human experts—Claude accelerates drafts, not accountability.
Ungrounded use on private or fast-changing facts invites confident wrong answers; grounding adds engineering and content ops.
Automation without human-in-the-loop gates (auto-send email, auto-close tickets) creates reputational and compliance risk.
Token cost and latency scale with volume—batch summarization of thousands of records needs API budgeting, not ad-hoc chat.
